questions.question: What is the study of the internal structure of plants called?
questions.explanation: Study of internal structure of plants is called anatomy.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: On the basis of their structure and location, how many types of tissue systems exist in plants?
questions.explanation: On the basis of their structure and location, there are three types of tissue systems. These are the epidermal tissue system, the ground or fundamental tissue system and the vascular or conducting tissue system.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the epidermal tissue system?
questions.explanation: The epidermal tissue system forms the outer-most covering of the whole plant body and comprises epidermal cells, stomata and the epidermal appendages - the trichomes and hairs.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which part of the plant lacks a cuticle?
questions.explanation: The outside of the epidermis is often covered with a waxy thick layer called the cuticle which prevents the loss of water. Cuticle is absent in roots.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the shape of guard cells in the stomata of grasses?
questions.explanation: In grasses, the guard cells are dumb-bell shaped.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which characteristic is true for the walls of guard cells?
options: Both inner and outer walls are thin
Both inner and outer walls are highly thickened
The outer walls are highly thickened and inner walls are thin
The outer walls are thin and the inner walls are highly thickened
questions.explanation: The outer walls of guard cells (away from the stomatal pore) are thin and the inner walls (towards the stomatal pore) are highly thickened.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What are the specialised epidermal cells found in the vicinity of guard cells known as?
questions.explanation: Sometimes, a few epidermal cells, in the vicinity of the guard cells become specialised in their shape and size and are known as subsidiary cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The stomatal apparatus is composed of which of the following?
options: Stomatal aperture, guard cells, and mesophyll cells
Stomatal aperture, guard cells, and subsidiary cells
Guard cells, subsidiary cells, and bundle sheath cells
Stomatal aperture, subsidiary cells, and trichomes
questions.explanation: The stomatal aperture, guard cells and the surrounding subsidiary cells are together called stomatal apparatus.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Root hairs are specifically described as:
questions.explanation: The root hairs are unicellular elongations of the epidermal cells and help absorb water and minerals from the soil.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the primary function of trichomes in the shoot system?
options: Preventing water loss due to transpiration
questions.explanation: The trichomes help in preventing water loss due to transpiration.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which tissues constitute the ground tissue system?
options: Only simple tissues like parenchyma and collenchyma
All tissues except epidermis and vascular bundles
questions.explanation: All tissues except epidermis and vascular bundles constitute the ground tissue.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In leaves, the ground tissue consisting of thin-walled chloroplast containing cells is called:
questions.explanation: In leaves, the ground tissue consists of thin-walled chloroplast containing cells and is called mesophyll.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Vascular bundles that possess cambium between phloem and xylem are termed as:
questions.explanation: Such vascular bundles because of the presence of cambium possess the ability to form secondary xylem and phloem tissues, and hence are called open vascular bundles.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In which plants do vascular bundles lack cambium and fail to form secondary tissues?
questions.explanation: In the monocotyledons, the vascular bundles have no cambium present in them. Hence, since they do not form secondary tissues they are referred to as closed.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the arrangement called when xylem and phloem within a vascular bundle are arranged in an alternate manner along different radii?
questions.explanation: When xylem and phloem within a vascular bundle are arranged in an alternate manner along the different radii, the arrangement is called radial such as in roots.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Where are conjoint vascular bundles commonly found?
questions.explanation: In conjoint type of vascular bundles, the xylem and phloem are jointly situated along the same radius of vascular bundles. Such vascular bundles are common in stems and leaves.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the outermost layer of a dicotyledonous root called?
questions.explanation: The outermost layer is epiblema. Many of the cells of epiblema protrude in the form of unicellular root hairs.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which cells in the dicot root feature a deposition of water-impermeable, waxy material called suberin?
questions.explanation: The tangential as well as radial walls of the endodermal cells have a deposition of water-impermeable, waxy material suberin in the form of casparian strips.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Where does the initiation of lateral roots and vascular cambium during secondary growth take place in dicot roots?
questions.explanation: Next to endodermis lies a few layers of thick-walled parenchyomatous cells referred to as pericycle. Initiation of lateral roots and vascular cambium during the secondary growth takes place in these cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The parenchymatous cells which lie between the xylem and the phloem in dicot roots are called:
questions.explanation: The parenchymatous cells which lie between the xylem and the phloem are called conjuctive tissue.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the stele?
questions.explanation: All tissues on the innerside of the endodermis such as pericycle, vascular bundles and pith constitute the stele.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Compared to a dicot root, a monocot root usually has:
questions.explanation: As compared to the dicot root which have fewer xylem bundles, there are usually more than six (polyarch) xylem bundles in the monocot root.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is true for a monocotyledonous root?
options: It has a large and well developed pith
It does not have an endodermis
questions.explanation: Pith is large and well developed. Monocotyledonous roots do not undergo any secondary growth.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a typical young dicotyledonous stem, what kind of cells make up the hypodermis?
questions.explanation: The outer hypodermis, consists of a few layers of collenchymatous cells just below the epidermis, which provide mechanical strength to the young stem.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The endodermis in a dicotyledonous stem is also referred to as the:
questions.explanation: The cells of the endodermis are rich in starch grains and the layer is also referred to as the starch sheath.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a dicot stem, the pericycle is present in the form of:
questions.explanation: Pericycle is present on the inner side of the endodermis and above the phloem in the form of semi-lunar patches of sclerenchyma.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic feature of a dicot stem?
questions.explanation: A large number of vascular bundles are arranged in a ring; the 'ring' arrangement of vascular bundles is a characteristic of dicot stem.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What type of vascular bundle is found in a dicot stem?
questions.explanation: Each vascular bundle is conjoint, open, and with endarch protoxylem.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The central portion of the dicot stem, which constitutes the pith, is made up of:
options: Parenchymatous cells with large intercellular spaces
questions.explanation: A large number of rounded, parenchymatous cells with large intercellular spaces which occupy the central portion of the stem constitute the pith.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a monocot stem, the hypodermis is made up of which type of tissue?
questions.explanation: The monocot stem has a sclerenchymatous hypodermis, a large number of scattered vascular bundles...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is true for the vascular bundles of a monocot stem?
options: They are arranged in a ring
They are scattered and each is surrounded by a sclerenchymatous bundle sheath
Peripheral vascular bundles are generally larger than centrally located ones
questions.explanation: The monocot stem has a sclerenchymatous hypodermis, a large number of scattered vascular bundles, each surrounded by a sclerenchymatous bundle sheath...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In monocot stems, which sizes of vascular bundles are generally smaller?
questions.explanation: Peripheral vascular bundles are generally smaller than the centrally located ones.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which structure is notably absent in the vascular bundles of a monocot stem?
questions.explanation: The phloem parenchyma is absent, and water-containing cavities are present within the vascular bundles.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the upper surface of a dorsiventral leaf called?
questions.explanation: The epidermis which covers both the upper surface (adaxial epidermis) and lower surface (abaxial epidermis) of the leaf has a conspicuous cuticle.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a dorsiventral leaf, which epidermis generally bears more stomata?
questions.explanation: The abaxial epidermis generally bears more stomata than the adaxial epidermis. The latter may even lack stomata.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What type of tissue makes up the mesophyll in a leaf?
questions.explanation: Mesophyll, which possesses chloroplasts and carry out photosynthesis, is made up of parenchyma.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a dicotyledonous leaf, the adaxially placed parenchyma consisting of vertically elongated cells is called:
questions.explanation: The adaxially placed palisade parenchyma is made up of elongated cells, which are arranged vertically and parallel to each other.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which mesophyll cells in a dorsiventral leaf are oval or round and loosely arranged with large spaces between them?
questions.explanation: The oval or round and loosely arranged spongy parenchyma is situated below the palisade cells and extends to the lower epidermis. There are numerous large spaces and air cavities between these cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In the reticulate venation of dicot leaves, the size of the vascular bundles is dependent on the:
questions.explanation: The size of the vascular bundles are dependent on the size of the veins. The veins vary in thickness in the reticulate venation of the dicot leaves.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In a dicot leaf, what surrounds the vascular bundles?
options: A layer of thin-walled mesophyll cells
A layer of thick walled bundle sheath cells
questions.explanation: The vascular bundles are surrounded by a layer of thick walled bundle sheath cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How are the stomata distributed in an isobilateral (monocotyledonous) leaf?
options: Present on both surfaces of the epidermis
questions.explanation: In an isobilateral leaf, the stomata are present on both the surfaces of the epidermis;
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In an isobilateral leaf, how is the mesophyll structured?
options: Differentiated into palisade and spongy parenchyma
Not differentiated into palisade and spongy parenchyma
questions.explanation: and the mesophyll is not differentiated into palisade and spongy parenchyma.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In grasses, certain adaxial epidermal cells along the veins modify themselves into large, empty, colourless cells called:
questions.explanation: In grasses, certain adaxial epidermal cells along the veins modify themselves into large, empty, colourless cells. These are called bulliform cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the function of bulliform cells when they are flaccid due to water stress?
options: They make the leaves curl inwards to minimise water loss
questions.explanation: When they are flaccid due to water stress, they make the leaves curl inwards to minimise water loss.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What causes the leaf surface of an isobilateral leaf to be exposed?
options: When bulliform cells absorb water and become turgid
When bulliform cells lose water and become flaccid
questions.explanation: When the bulliform cells in the leaves have absorbed water and are turgid, the leaf surface is exposed.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How is parallel venation in monocot leaves reflected in the vertical sections of the leaves?
options: Vascular bundles are of near similar sizes (except in main veins)
There is only one large central vascular bundle
questions.explanation: The parallel venation in monocot leaves is reflected in the near similar sizes of vascular bundles (except in main veins) as seen in vertical sections of the leaves.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which tissue system forms the main bulk of the plant?
questions.explanation: The ground tissue system forms the main bulk of the plant.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The ground tissue system is divided into which three zones?
questions.explanation: It is divided into three zones - cortex, pericycle and pith.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Secondary growth occurs in most of the:
questions.explanation: The secondary growth occurs in most of the dicotyledonous roots and stems.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which parts of a plant show adaptations to diverse environments through variations in their internal structures?
options: Different organs including roots, stems, and leaves
questions.explanation: Different organs in a plant show differences in their internal structure. [...] Internal structures also show adaptations to diverse environments.
questions.subject: Biology
